sanskrit: न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते    नान्तो न चादिर्न च संप्रतिष्ठा । अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूल    मसङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा ॥
hindiTranslation: इस संसारवृक्षका जैसा रूप देखनेमें आता है, वैसा यहाँ विचार करनेपर मिलता नहीं क्योंकि इसका न तो आदि है, न अन्त है और न स्थिति ही है ।  इसलिये इस दृढ़ मूलोंवाले संसाररूप अश्वत्थवृक्षको दृढ़ असङ्गतारूप शस्त्रके द्वारा काटकर ॥
englishTranslation: Its form is not perceived here as it truly is: it has no beginning, no end, and no fixed base. Cut down this deeply rooted ashvattha tree with the strong sword of detachment.
essence: What traps you has no fixed form; detachment cuts it cleanly.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The world-tree cannot be grasped as a normal thing, because it has no clear beginning, end, or stable base. Cut through it with firm detachment.
simpleInsight: What looks solid loses its grip when you stop clinging to it.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, with Arjuna frozen between duty and fear, Krishna explains the world as a vast tree of changing appearances. This verse gives the method: do not try to master it by grasping it; cut through attachment itself.
modernRelevance: You keep replaying the breakup, the job offer, the family argument, trying to find the exact point where everything became real. The mind wants a fixed story; this verse says the story itself must be cut loose.
translationLiteral: न (not) / रूपम् (form) / अस्य (of this) / इह (here) / तथा (thus) / उपलभ्यते (is perceived) / न (not) / अन्तः (end) / न (not) / च (and) / आदिः (beginning) / न (not) / च (and) / संप्रतिष्ठा (fixed base) / अश्वत्थम् (ashvattha tree) / एनम् (this) / सु-विरूढ-मूलम् (deeply rooted) / असङ्ग-शस्त्रेण (with the weapon of detachment) / दृढेन (strong) / छित्त्वा (having cut)
